revision.text:
{{神社| 画像=| 名称=虫鹿神社| 所在地=愛知県犬山市前原向屋敷62| 位置=| 祭神=[[国常立尊]] 国狹槌尊 豊斗渟尊 [[天照大神]] [[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]、旧村社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事=}}
'''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。
== 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。
元々は尾張国丹羽郡入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために灌漑用溜池（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。
[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。
現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）、村社となる。
== 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]]|
== 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市前原向屋敷62
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜バスコミュニティ]]明治村線・長者町団地線「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]より「[[博物館明治村|明治村]]」「長者町団地」行き
{{shinto-stub}}
[[Category:愛知県の神社|むしかしんしや]] [[Category:犬山市|むしかしんしや]]

{{神社| 画像=| 名称=虫鹿神社| 所在地=愛知県犬山市前原向屋敷62| 位置=| 祭神=[[国常立尊]] 国狹槌尊 豊斗渟尊 [[天照大神]] [[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]、旧村社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事=}}

[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。

{{神社| 画像=[[File:Musika jinja.JPG|250px]]| 名称=虫鹿神社| 所在地=[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]前原向屋敷62| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|}}| 祭神=[[国常立尊]]
国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
[[天照大神]]
[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]、旧村社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事=}} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は尾張国丹羽郡入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために灌漑用溜池（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（明治元年）、村社となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]]| == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市前原向屋敷62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜バスコミュニティ]]明治村線・長者町団地線「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]より「[[博物館明治村|明治村]]」「長者町団地」行き {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]]

{{神社| 画像=[[File:Musika jinja.JPG|250px]]| 名称=虫鹿神社| 所在地=[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]前原向屋敷62| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|}}| 祭神=[[国常立尊]]
国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
[[天照大神]]

[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]、旧村社 | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]][[入鹿村]]に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために[[灌漑]]用[[溜池]]（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[村社]]となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市[[前原向屋敷]]62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜バスコミュニティ]]明治村線・長者町団地線「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 ** [[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]より「[[博物館明治村|明治村]]」「長者町団地」行き {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mushika Shrine'''|虫鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{神社 | 名称=虫鹿神社 | 画像=[[File:Musika jinja.JPG|250px]]
鳥居 | 所在地=[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]前原向屋敷62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神=[[国常立尊]]
国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
[[天照大神]]
[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために[[灌漑]]用[[溜池]]（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[村社]]となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市[[前原向屋敷]]62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]][[博物館明治村|明治村線]]「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

{{神社 | 名称=虫鹿神社 | 画像=[[File:Musika jinja.JPG|250px]]
鳥居 | 所在地=[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]前原向屋敷62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神=[[国常立尊]]
国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
[[天照大神]]
[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために[[灌漑]]用[[溜池]]（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[村社]]となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市[[前原向屋敷]]62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]][[博物館明治村|明治村線]]「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために[[灌漑]]用[[溜池]]（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[村社]]となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市[[前原向屋敷]]62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]][[博物館明治村|明治村線]]「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]）である。 == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は[[尾張国]][[丹羽郡]]入鹿村に鎮座していた。[[江戸時代]]初期、[[尾張藩]]の新田開発のために[[灌漑]]用[[溜池]]（[[入鹿池]]）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、[[1635年]]（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[村社]]となる。 == 祭神 == *[[国常立尊]] *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *[[天照大神]] *[[菊理媛神|菊理媛命]] == 所在地 == *愛知県犬山市[[前原向屋敷]]62 == 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]][[博物館明治村|明治村線]]「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

jinja.JPG|250px]]
鳥居 | 所在地={{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|犬山市|Q503411}}前原向屋敷62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神={{jalink|国常立尊}}
国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}
{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|犬山市|Q503411}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}）である。 === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}入鹿村に鎮座していた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期、{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}の新田開発のために{{jalink|灌漑|Q11453}}用{{jalink|溜池}}（{{jalink|入鹿池|Q927013}}）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 {{jalink|1632年|Q6865}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、{{jalink|1635年|Q6879}}（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}となる。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|国常立尊}} *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} === 所在地 === *愛知県犬山市{{jalink|前原向屋敷}}62 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}{{jalink|博物館明治村|Q985290|明治村線}}「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

国狹槌尊
豊斗渟尊
{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}
{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|犬山市|Q503411}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}）である。 === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}入鹿村に鎮座していた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期、{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}の新田開発のために{{jalink|灌漑|Q11453}}用{{jalink|溜池}}（{{jalink|入鹿池|Q927013}}）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 {{jalink|1632年|Q6865}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、{{jalink|1635年|Q6879}}（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}となる。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|国常立尊}} *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} === 所在地 === *愛知県犬山市{{jalink|前原向屋敷}}62 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}{{jalink|博物館明治村|Q985290|明治村線}}「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|犬山市|Q503411}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}）である。 === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}入鹿村に鎮座していた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期、{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}の新田開発のために{{jalink|灌漑|Q11453}}用{{jalink|溜池}}（{{jalink|入鹿池|Q927013}}）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 {{jalink|1632年|Q6865}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、{{jalink|1635年|Q6879}}（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}となる。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|国常立尊}} *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} === 所在地 === *愛知県犬山市{{jalink|前原向屋敷}}62 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}{{jalink|博物館明治村|Q985290|明治村線}}「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=虫鹿神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''虫鹿神社'''（むしかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|犬山市|Q503411}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}）である。 === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。虫鹿天神、三明神社とも称していたという。 元々は{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|丹羽郡|Q1347719}}入鹿村に鎮座していた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期、{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}の新田開発のために{{jalink|灌漑|Q11453}}用{{jalink|溜池}}（{{jalink|入鹿池|Q927013}}）が建設されることになり、入鹿村は水没することになる。入鹿村の人々は周辺地域に移住となり、虫鹿神社も移転となる。 {{jalink|1632年|Q6865}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}9年）、入鹿池造成が開始される。虫鹿神社は入鹿村の人々が最も多く移住した場所である前原新田への移転が決まり、{{jalink|1635年|Q6879}}（寛永12年）に現在地に遷座する。 現在の社殿はこの移転時の建物という。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}となる。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|国常立尊}} *国狹槌尊 *豊斗渟尊 *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理媛命}} === 所在地 === *愛知県犬山市{{jalink|前原向屋敷}}62 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}{{jalink|博物館明治村|Q985290|明治村線}}「前原新向」バス停下車、徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii | 所在地={{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Inuyama|Q503411}} Maehara Mukoyashiki 62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神={{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}

{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Inuyama|Q503411}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, for new field development under the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{jalink|Iruka Pond|Q927013}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{jalink|1632|Q6865}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{jalink|1635|Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{jalink|Maehara Mukoyashiki}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{jalink|Meijimura|Q985290|Meijimura}} Line of {{jalink|Gifu Bus|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

Torii | 所在地={{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Inuyama|Q503411}} Maehara Mukoyashiki 62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神={{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}

{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Inuyama|Q503411}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, for new field development under the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{jalink|Iruka Pond|Q927013}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{jalink|1632|Q6865}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{jalink|1635|Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{jalink|Maehara Mukoyashiki}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{jalink|Meijimura|Q985290|Meijimura}} Line of {{jalink|Gifu Bus|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, {{jalink|Niwa District|Q1347719}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, for new field development under the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{jalink|Iruka Pond|Q927013}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{jalink|1632|Q6865}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{jalink|1635|Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{jalink|Maehara Mukoyashiki}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{jalink|Meijimura|Q985290|Meijimura}} Line of {{jalink|Gifu Bus|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]]

The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{jalink|1632|Q6865}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{jalink|1635|Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{jalink|Maehara Mukoyashiki}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{jalink|Meijimura|Q985290|Meijimura}} Line of {{jalink|Gifu Bus|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411}} Maehara Mukoyashiki 62 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|44.72|N|136|58|23.46|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=虫鹿神社}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto
Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むしかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=unknown | 本殿= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mushika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mushika Shrine''' (むしかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}). === Overview === Its founding period is unknown. It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|WD=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|WD=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|WD=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|WD=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi]]

It was also called Mushika Tenjin and Sanmyōjin Shrine. Originally it stood in Iruka village, Niwa District, Owari Province. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, for new field development under the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|qid=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}, an irrigation reservoir ({{ill|lt=Iruka Pond|Q927013|qid=Q927013|1=Lake Iruka}}) was built, and Iruka village was submerged. The people of Iruka village relocated to surrounding areas, and Mushika Shrine was also relocated. In {{ill|lt=1632|Q6865|qid=Q6865|1=1632}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 9), construction of Iruka Pond began. The shrine was decided to move to Maehara Shinden, where the largest number of Iruka villagers relocated, and in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|qid=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kan'ei 12) it was transferred to the current site. The current shrine building is said to date from that relocation. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} *Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto *Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} === Location === *62 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maehara Mukoyashiki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Inuyama, Aichi === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from "Maehara Shinkō" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meijimura|Q985290|qid=Q985290|lt=Meijimura|1=Meiji-mura}} Line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mushika Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi]]
